narrative_thread: Player has removed the oversized T-shirt, leaving them topless in the bathroom. The lacy red underwear remains the only thing covering their lower half, though its elastic is completely shot.
blueprint: Living Room (ID: room_091a9830) is now fully realized. Future updates: Patio (east from kitchen, south from living room).

player.description: You’re standing in the middle of the bathroom, completely topless, with the lacy red underwear clinging to your hips. The elastic is shot, the fabric is grimy, and the whole situation feels like a metaphor for your life right now. Your hair is a wild halo of kinks, your dark circles are deep enough to qualify as topographical features, and the fluorescent light is doing absolutely nothing to flatter your current state. Eve the cat watches from her damp towel nest, her judgment palpable.

room_start.name: Bedroom
room_start.description:
Your bedroom is a sanctuary of controlled chaos. The walls are painted a soothing lavender, though the effect is somewhat ruined by the pile of discarded clothes in the corner. A queen-sized bed, unmade and rumpled, dominates the space, its sheets still smelling faintly of last night’s perfume and regret. A nightstand sits beside it, cluttered with half-empty water glasses and a bottle of painkillers. To the east, a closet door stands slightly ajar, revealing a sliver of darkness within. The door to the hallway is closed to the west, but you can hear the faint hum of the refrigerator from beyond. A window to the south lets in a sliver of sunlight, illuminating the dust motes dancing in the air.

You can see here: Eve, nightstand, half-empty glass of water, ibuprofen, lip balm, loose change, closet, bed, pillow, window, bedroom door, lacy bra with a broken clasp, oversized T-shirt.

The walls are painted a soothing lavender, though the effect is somewhat ruined by the pile of discarded clothes in the corner. A queen-sized bed, unmade and rumpled, dominates the space, its sheets still smelling faintly of last night’s perfume and regret. A nightstand sits beside it, cluttered with half-empty water glasses and a bottle of painkillers. To the east, a closet door stands slightly ajar, revealing a sliver of darkness within. The door to the hallway is closed to the west, but you can hear the faint hum of the refrigerator from beyond. A window to the south lets in a sliver of sunlight, illuminating the dust motes dancing in the air.

room_b62717ab.name: Hallway
room_b62717ab.description:
The hallway is a narrow passage that stretches north and south, connecting the various rooms of the house. The walls are painted a neutral beige, adorned with a few framed photos of you and your friends from nights out—some of them a little too blurry to be flattering. The hardwood floor creaks softly underfoot, a familiar sound that usually means you’re home. To the north, the hallway opens into what looks like the bathroom, its door slightly ajar. To the south, you can see the closed door of your bedroom. To the east, a doorway leads into the kitchen, where the hum of the refrigerator grows louder. To the west, a staircase descends into darkness, the steps disappearing into shadow. The air smells faintly of last night’s takeout and the lingering scent of your perfume.

You can see here: framed photo, hallway rug, takeout container, perfume bottle, lacy black thong.
room_b62717ab.base_description: The hallway is a narrow passage that stretches north and south, connecting the various rooms of the house. The walls are painted a neutral beige, adorned with a few framed photos of you and your friends from nights out—some of them a little too blurry to be flattering. The hardwood floor creaks softly underfoot, a familiar sound that usually means you’re home. To the north, the hallway opens into what looks like the bathroom, its door slightly ajar. To the south, you can see the closed door of your bedroom. To the east, a doorway leads into the kitchen, where the hum of the refrigerator grows louder. To the west, a staircase descends into darkness, the steps disappearing into shadow. The air smells faintly of last night’s takeout and the lingering scent of your perfume.

room_202925de.name: Walk-In Closet
room_202925de.description:
The walk-in closet is a narrow, dimly lit space that smells faintly of cedar and old perfume. The walls are lined with wire shelving units, sagging slightly under the weight of folded clothes, shoeboxes, and an assortment of forgotten accessories. A single bare bulb hangs from the ceiling, casting long shadows over the clutter. To the west, the closet door you just stepped through leads back to your bedroom. A rickety wooden rod runs along the north wall, sagging under the weight of hangers—some empty, some still bearing the ghosts of outfits past. The air is thick with the scent of fabric softener and the faint musk of leather from the few pairs of boots shoved into the corner. A full-length mirror leans against the east wall, its surface smudged with fingerprints and dust. The floor is a minefield of discarded heels, sneakers, and the occasional lone sock.

You can see here: wire shelving unit, full-length mirror, wooden rod, pile of shoes, cedar-lined shoebox, crumpled receipt.
room_202925de.base_description: The walk-in closet is a narrow, dimly lit space that smells faintly of cedar and old perfume. The walls are lined with wire shelving units, sagging slightly under the weight of folded clothes, shoeboxes, and an assortment of forgotten accessories. A single bare bulb hangs from the ceiling, casting long shadows over the clutter. To the west, the closet door you just stepped through leads back to your bedroom. A rickety wooden rod runs along the north wall, sagging under the weight of hangers—some empty, some still bearing the ghosts of outfits past. The air is thick with the scent of fabric softener and the faint musk of leather from the few pairs of boots shoved into the corner. A full-length mirror leans against the east wall, its surface smudged with fingerprints and dust. The floor is a minefield of discarded heels, sneakers, and the occasional lone sock.

room_0e6f7714.name: Bathroom
room_0e6f7714.description:
The bathroom is a cramped but functional space, bathed in the harsh, unflattering glow of a fluorescent light that hums faintly overhead. The walls are tiled in a pale mint green that hasn’t aged well, the grout between them stained and slightly yellowed. A foggy mirror hangs above a porcelain sink, its surface speckled with toothpaste splatters and a few stray hairs. The air is thick with the scent of damp towels, lavender-scented soap, and the faint tang of last night’s tequila still clinging to the back of your throat. To the south, the hallway beckons, its familiar creaks and scents a stark contrast to the sterile, slightly claustrophobic atmosphere here. The linoleum floor is cold underfoot, a thin bath mat offering little comfort. A shower curtain, patterned with faded seashells, is drawn halfway across the tub, its plastic rustling softly as you move.

You can see here: foggy mirror, lavender soap, damp towel, seashell shower curtain, medicine cabinet, crumpled cash, lacy red underwear, self.
room_0e6f7714.base_description: The bathroom is a cramped but functional space, bathed in the harsh, unflattering glow of a fluorescent light that hums faintly overhead. The walls are tiled in a pale mint green that hasn’t aged well, the grout between them stained and slightly yellowed. A foggy mirror hangs above a porcelain sink, its surface speckled with toothpaste splatters and a few stray hairs. The air is thick with the scent of damp towels, lavender-scented soap, and the faint tang of last night’s tequila still clinging to the back of your throat. To the south, the hallway beckons, its familiar creaks and scents a stark contrast to the sterile, slightly claustrophobic atmosphere here. The linoleum floor is cold underfoot, a thin bath mat offering little comfort. A shower curtain, patterned with faded seashells, is drawn halfway across the tub, its plastic rustling softly as you move.

room_5f3b3e3d.name: Kitchen
room_5f3b3e3d.description:
The kitchen is a small but functional space, bathed in the harsh glow of a fluorescent light that hums softly overhead. The linoleum floor, a checkerboard of white and faded yellow tiles, is cool underfoot. To the west, the hallway beckons, its neutral tones a stark contrast to the clutter here. The countertops, a speckled laminate, are littered with the remnants of last night’s takeout and a few unwashed dishes. The air is thick with the scent of stale coffee, grease, and the faint tang of something slightly sour—maybe the milk in the fridge is on its way out. The refrigerator, a white monolith, stands against the far wall, its surface covered in magnets, receipts, and a few photos held up by novelty bottle-opener magnets. To the east, a back door leads out to a small patio, though the blinds are drawn, casting the outside in shadow. The sink is piled high with dishes, and the dish rack is overflowing, a testament to your lack of motivation this morning. A small window above the sink lets in a sliver of daylight, illuminating the dust motes dancing in the air.

You can see here: refrigerator, coffee maker, takeout containers, dish rack.

The air is thick with the scent of stale coffee, grease, and the faint tang of something slightly sour—maybe the milk in the fridge is on its way out. The refrigerator, a white monolith, stands against the far wall, its surface covered in magnets, receipts, and a few photos held up by novelty bottle-opener magnets. To the east, a back door leads out to a small patio, though the blinds are drawn, casting the outside in shadow. The sink is piled high with dishes, and the dish rack is overflowing, a testament to your lack of motivation this morning. A small window above the sink lets in a sliver of daylight, illuminating the dust motes dancing in the air.

room_091a9830.name: Living Room
room_091a9830.description:
The staircase deposits you into a dimly lit living room, the kind of space that’s seen too many late nights and not enough cleaning. The air is thick with the scent of stale beer, cigarette smoke, and the faint musk of last night’s perfume still clinging to the furniture. A sagging, overstuffed couch sits against the far wall, its cushions indented from countless hours of use—some of them yours. A coffee table, littered with empty glasses, a half-empty bottle of cheap whiskey, and a scattering of crumpled dollar bills, dominates the center of the room. The walls are adorned with a few tacky posters of 90s rock bands and a framed mirror that’s seen better days, its edges slightly tarnished. To the east, a doorway leads back to the staircase and the hallway above. To the south, a sliding glass door offers a glimpse of the patio outside, though the blinds are drawn, casting the room in a muted, grayish light. The hardwood floor is cold underfoot, and the faint hum of the refrigerator from the kitchen is still audible, though distant.

You can see here: overstuffed couch, coffee table, cheap whiskey bottle, crumpled dollar bills, sliding glass door.

The air is thick with the scent of stale beer, cigarette smoke, and the faint musk of last night’s perfume still clinging to the furniture. A sagging, overstuffed couch sits against the far wall, its cushions indented from countless hours of use—some of them yours. A coffee table, littered with empty glasses, a half-empty bottle of cheap whiskey, and a scattering of crumpled dollar bills, dominates the center of the room. The walls are adorned with a few tacky posters of 90s rock bands and a framed mirror that’s seen better days, its edges slightly tarnished. To the east, a doorway leads back to the staircase and the hallway above. To the south, a sliding glass door offers a glimpse of the patio outside, though the blinds are drawn, casting the room in a muted, grayish light. The hardwood floor is cold underfoot, and the faint hum of the refrigerator from the kitchen is still audible, though distant.

i_a73a47.name: self
i_a73a47.description: You’re still perched on the closed toilet lid, though now you’ve just finished peeing, which should feel like a victory but somehow doesn’t. Your oversized T-shirt is riding up even higher, exposing more of your thighs, and your posture is even more slumped than before, like your body is actively trying to collapse in on itself. Your dark circles are now *officially* dark craters, and your hair is a wild, staticky halo around your head. The fluorescent light overhead is doing you no favors, casting shadows under your eyes that make you look like you’ve been up for days—which, let’s be honest, you have. Eve the cat is still watching you from her damp towel nest, her tail flicking in what you *choose* to interpret as sympathy rather than judgment.

i_678314.name: oversized T-shirt
i_678314.description: A thin, oversized T-shirt that barely covers your thighs. It’s soft from years of wear, with a faded logo for a band you don’t even listen to anymore. The fabric is slightly wrinkled, and it smells faintly of laundry detergent and last night’s perfume.

i_bec0b4.name: lacy black thong
i_bec0b4.description: A lacy black thong, now discarded on the hallway floor near the rug. The elastic is slightly stretched, and the lace is frayed at the edges—evidence of a night well spent (or poorly survived). It smells faintly of sweat, perfume, and the kind of regret that only comes from dancing on tables at 3 AM.

i_40c5fd.name: crumpled cash
i_40c5fd.description: A wad of damp, crumpled bills—mostly singles and fives—with a lone twenty tucked in the middle. The money smells faintly of tequila, perfume, and the lingering musk of last night’s work. The total adds up to **$87**, which is either a small fortune or a sad commentary on your life choices, depending on how you look at it. The bills are slightly sticky, clinging to each other like they’re afraid to be separated.

i_c2bade.name: Eve
i_c2bade.description: A sleek black cat with eyes like polished emeralds. Eve is currently stretched out at the foot of the bed, her tail flicking lazily. She’s just been kissed on the forehead, and while she’s still judging you, there’s a faint air of tolerance in her posture. Her purring is steady, though she’s clearly trying to maintain her dignity after such a display of affection.

i_cf6644.name: nightstand
i_cf6644.description: A small, wooden nightstand beside your bed. It’s cluttered with the detritus of daily life: a half-empty glass of water, a bottle of ibuprofen, a tube of lip balm, and a scattering of loose change. The drawer is slightly ajar, revealing a tangle of charging cables and a forgotten romance novel.

i_0e18ea.name: half-empty glass of water
i_0e18ea.description: A glass of water that’s now even more half-empty after your impromptu pill-chasing. The water is still lukewarm and slightly stale, but at least it served its purpose. The glass is smudged with fingerprints, a testament to your current state of disarray.

i_8d538d.name: ibuprofen
i_8d538d.description: A nearly empty bottle of ibuprofen. The label is slightly faded, and the cap is still askew. There are only a couple of pills left rattling around inside—enough for one more dose, maybe two if you’re desperate. You’ll have to restock soon, or the next hangover is going to be brutal.

i_c15f2a.name: lip balm
i_c15f2a.description: A nearly empty tube of cherry-flavored lip balm. The rubber band you used to keep it from drying out is now loose around the tube, and the balm inside is barely a smear. It’s almost time to toss this and buy a new one—if you remember.

i_849375.name: loose change
i_849375.description: A scattering of coins on the nightstand: a few quarters, dimes, and pennies. It’s not much, but it might be enough to buy a coffee or a snack later. The coins are slightly tarnished, as if they’ve been sitting there for a while.

i_b66f74.name: closet
i_b66f74.description: A walk-in closet, its door slightly ajar. Inside, you can see a mess of clothes hanging on racks and piled on the floor. There’s a faint smell of fabric softener and old perfume. A full-length mirror leans against one wall, reflecting the chaos within.

i_93d81d.name: bed
i_93d81d.description: A queen-sized bed with a rumpled comforter and sheets that smell faintly of perfume and sweat. The mattress is slightly lumpy, but it’s yours, and it’s comfortable enough—when you’re not aching from last night’s work. The pillows are askew, and one has fallen onto the floor.

i_6f25a3.name: pillow
i_6f25a3.description: A soft, slightly flattened pillow that’s seen better days. It’s covered in a faded floral case that’s starting to fray at the edges. The pillow smells faintly of your shampoo and last night’s hairspray.

i_b3106b.name: window
i_b3106b.description: A large window with blinds that are slightly askew, letting in a sliver of sunlight. The blinds are dusty, and the window itself is smudged with fingerprints. Outside, you can see your small backyard, overgrown with weeds and a rusted patio set. The view isn’t much, but it’s yours.

i_a668fd.name: bedroom door
i_a668fd.description: A plain wooden door leading to the hallway. It’s currently closed, muffling the sounds of the house beyond. The doorknob is slightly loose, and the door itself has a few scratches near the bottom—courtesy of Eve’s claws.

i_fdb7ab.name: wire shelving unit
i_fdb7ab.description: A utilitarian wire shelving unit bolted to the wall. It’s packed with folded sweaters, jeans, and a few shoeboxes labeled in your messy handwriting. One of the shelves is slightly bent, as if something heavy was once placed on it.

i_613716.name: full-length mirror
i_613716.description: A tall, freestanding mirror with a wooden frame. The glass is smudged and slightly warped at the edges, but it reflects your tired face back at you with unflinching honesty. The mirror leans precariously against the wall, as if it might topple over if you breathe too hard. Right now, it’s showing you a woman who looks like she’s been through the wringer—oversized T-shirt, wild hair, and all.

i_27ad37.name: wooden rod
i_27ad37.description: A flimsy wooden rod stretched across the north wall, holding an assortment of hangers. Some are empty, swaying slightly in the draft from the open door. Others still cling to the remnants of outfits—sequined tops, a few pairs of jeans, and a lone bathrobe that’s seen better days.

i_bb1303.name: pile of shoes
i_bb1303.description: A chaotic heap of footwear in the southeast corner of the closet. Stilettos, sneakers, and a few pairs of scuffed work boots are jumbled together, as if someone (you) kicked them off in a hurry. A lone sock peeks out from beneath a black leather boot.

i_885a20.name: cedar-lined shoebox
i_885a20.description: A small, rectangular shoebox with a cedar lining. The lid is now fully open, revealing its contents: a few pairs of delicate underwear, a lacy bra with a broken clasp, and a crumpled receipt from a lingerie store. The cedar scent is strong, and the tissue paper inside is slightly yellowed with age. A lone receipt lies on the floor nearby.

i_c3780a.name: crumpled receipt
i_c3780a.description: A small, crumpled receipt from a lingerie store, dated over a year ago. It’s for a lacy bra that you apparently never returned. The ink is slightly faded, but the total is still legible: $29.99. It lies on the floor near the shoebox.

i_46958b.name: lacy red underwear
i_46958b.description: A pair of once-elegant red lace underwear, now stretched out, slightly grimy, and with the elastic completely shot. The satin bow at the waistband is frayed beyond recognition, and the fabric clings to your skin like it’s clinging to its last shred of purpose. It’s seen better days—*much* better days—but at this point, it’s more of a sentimental relic than actual underwear. The dampness from last night’s sweat and the ghost of last night’s perfume still cling to it, a sad little time capsule of your life choices.

i_3eb451.name: lacy bra with a broken clasp
aliases: lacy bra with a broken clasp
i_3eb451.description: A delicate black lace bra with a hint of shimmer, now abandoned on the bed. The clasp in the back is bent and useless, the straps slightly frayed. It lies there like a discarded promise, the scent of cedar still clinging to the fabric. Eve has already judged it—and you—for this act of surrender.

i_72ec64.name: framed photo
i_72ec64.description: A slightly blurry photo of you and your friends at a club, arms slung around each other. You’re all wearing matching feather boas and grinning like idiots. The frame is cheap plastic, but it’s held up surprisingly well.

i_4d9eea.name: hallway rug
i_4d9eea.description: A thin, threadbare runner rug that stretches the length of the hallway. It’s seen better days—frayed at the edges and stained in a few places—but it’s soft underfoot and muffles the creaking of the floorboards.

i_3bf61b.name: takeout container
i_3bf61b.description: A greasy cardboard container from last night’s late-night snack. It’s half-open, revealing the sad remains of a burrito—now cold and congealed. The smell is a mix of spices and regret.

i_970ca2.name: perfume bottle
i_970ca2.description: A half-empty bottle of your favorite perfume, a floral scent with a hint of vanilla. The cap is off, and the air around it is still lightly fragranced. You must have sprayed some last night before heading out.

i_6f4ca7.name: refrigerator
i_6f4ca7.description: A white, slightly dented refrigerator with a freezer compartment at the top. The door is covered in magnets, receipts, and a few photos. It hums quietly, a steady background noise in the kitchen. The handle is cool to the touch, and the seal around the door looks a little grimy.

i_7c2b98.name: coffee maker
i_7c2b98.description: A sleek, black coffee maker with a glass carafe half-full of cold, bitter coffee. The warming plate is still on, though it’s been hours since the last brew. The scent of stale coffee clings to it, mingling with the faint smell of burnt grounds in the filter.

i_fbbd72.name: takeout containers
i_fbbd72.description: A small pile of greasy takeout containers, some with lids still on, others open and revealing the congealed remains of last night’s dinner. The scent of soy sauce and fried rice lingers, mixed with the faint odor of something that’s seen better days. A few plastic forks and chopsticks are scattered among them.

i_f0ce7c.name: dish rack
i_f0ce7c.description: A plastic dish rack overflowing with clean but unput-away dishes. A few plates, bowls, and mugs teeter precariously, while a handful of silverware juts out at odd angles. A damp dish towel is draped over one side, its edges slightly stiff from dried water.

i_f1a4a8.name: foggy mirror
i_f1a4a8.description: The mirror is clouded with steam and streaks of dried toothpaste. Your reflection is a smudged, tired version of yourself, dark circles under your eyes and your hair a wild halo of kinks. It’s not exactly a confidence booster, but it’s honest.

i_16fabe.name: lavender soap
i_16fabe.description: A bar of lavender-scented soap, half-used and slightly squishy from sitting in a puddle of water. It smells like a spa, or at least what you imagine a spa would smell like if you could afford one. The wrapper is long gone, leaving the soap bare and a little sad.

i_598ebb.name: damp towel
i_598ebb.description: A plush, dark blue towel, damp and slightly musty from being left in a heap on the floor. It’s soft to the touch, but the dampness clings to your fingers, and you can practically hear your mother’s voice in your head: *‘Hang it up, sweetie, or it’ll never dry.’* Yeah, yeah.

i_b6c62c.name: seashell shower curtain
i_b6c62c.description: A plastic shower curtain covered in a pattern of seashells and starfish, their colors faded from years of use. It’s drawn halfway across the tub, the rings squeaking softly as you nudge it aside. The curtain clings to the side of the tub, damp and slightly sticky to the touch. Behind it, the tub is a mess of half-empty shampoo bottles and a loofah that’s seen better days.

i_03dc77.name: medicine cabinet
i_03dc77.description: A small, mirrored cabinet mounted above the sink. The door is slightly ajar, revealing a clutter of half-empty pill bottles, a tube of toothpaste, and a few stray bobby pins. The mirror on the inside of the door is just as foggy as the one outside, but you can still make out the reflection of your tired face staring back at you.

i_b3770d.name: toilet
i_b3770d.description: A standard porcelain toilet with a slightly chipped base and an upturned seat. The bowl is a murky blue-green, and the water inside sloshes uninvitingly. The tank hums ominously, and a half-empty roll of toilet paper hangs precariously from the holder. A sad, scentless air freshener clip dangles from the tank, a relic of better-smelling days. The faint scent of bleach lingers, but it’s no match for the underlying bathroom funk.

i_27199b.name: toilet paper
i_27199b.description: A half-empty roll of toilet paper, the end dangling like it’s one strong flush away from unraveling completely. The paper is slightly rough, the kind that makes you question your life choices if you’re not *very* careful.

i_ad12ca.name: air freshener clip
i_ad12ca.description: A plastic air freshener clip, long since emptied of its scent. It hangs limply from the toilet tank, a silent testament to the fact that no amount of artificial freshness can save this bathroom.

i_4909ff.name: bathroom
i_4909ff.description: The bathroom is still a cramped, slightly depressing space, but now it smells faintly of urine and lavender soap, a bizarre olfactory cocktail that somehow feels appropriate for your life. The foggy mirror is still foggy, your reflection still a smudged, tired mess. The toilet lid is back in place, though the seat is still slightly warm from your brief occupancy. The damp towel on the floor is now slightly more damp, thanks to the humidity from your *extensive* peeing session. Eve the cat hasn’t moved, but she does look slightly more judgmental than before. The seashell shower curtain rustles softly in the nonexistent breeze, and the fluorescent light hums overhead like a sad little lullaby.

i_888446.name: overstuffed couch
i_888446.description: A sagging, well-worn couch with a floral pattern that’s seen better decades. The cushions are lumpy, and one of the armrests has a suspicious stain that you’d rather not think about. It’s the kind of couch that’s comfortable in the way a hangover is comfortable—familiar, but not exactly pleasant.

i_cf1749.name: coffee table
i_cf1749.description: A low, wooden coffee table with a veneer that’s peeling at the edges. It’s covered in the detritus of last night: empty glasses, a bottle of whiskey with just a swallow left, and a few crumpled dollar bills that might have fallen out of your bra at some point. There’s also a remote control buried under the clutter, its buttons sticky with spilled alcohol.

i_2a935c.name: cheap whiskey bottle
i_2a935c.description: A half-empty bottle of bottom-shelf whiskey, the kind that burns going down and leaves you regretting it the next morning. There’s still a swallow or two left at the bottom, though you’re not sure you want to risk it.

i_bab3a6.name: crumpled dollar bills
i_bab3a6.description: A small pile of crumpled dollar bills, the kind that get stuffed into your underwear during a lap dance. They’re a little sweaty and worse for wear, but hey, money’s money.

i_dccd5c.name: sliding glass door
i_dccd5c.description: A large sliding glass door that leads out to the patio. The blinds are drawn, casting the room in a dim, diffused light, but you can still make out the faint outline of the backyard through the gaps. The door itself is slightly ajar, letting in a cool breeze that carries the scent of cut grass and morning dew.
